Astro + Go WASM + Service Worker

CertGen (offline) - generate your CA + server cert

Fill domain, ip and org. Then download a certs.zip with certs/.

Form

Certificate data

Validation runs in browser and in WASM backend to avoid generation errors.

DNS name used by the browser (must match exactly).

Server IP address (added to SAN).

Organization name written inside the certificate.

Asterisk • FreePBX • IssabelPBX • VitalPBX

Which file goes where?

These files are generated inside certs/.

server.crt
Server certificate (public part).
server.key
Server private key (secret).
ca.crt
CA that signed the server cert (chain).
ca.key
CA private key (VERY secret, do not upload).
fullchain.crt
Server cert + CA cert in one file (useful for some systems).
Certificate validity
  • CA certificate (ca.crt): 10 years
  • Server certificate (server.crt): 5 years

These validity periods are enforced by the WASM backend during generation.

VitalPBX example
  • Certificate: server.crt
  • Key: server.key
  • Chain: ca.crt

Use this mapping in platforms that ask for Certificate / Key / Chain fields (Asterisk, FreePBX, IssabelPBX, VitalPBX).

Install CA manually

Open a step-by-step modal to install ca.crt on each OS.

Install ca.crt

Install from PowerShell (without script)

  1. Open PowerShell as Administrator.
  2. Go to the extracted cert folder:
cd "C:\Users\$env:USERNAME\Downloads\certs"
  1. Install ca.crt:
Import-Certificate -FilePath ".\ca.crt" -CertStoreLocation "Cert:\LocalMachine\Root"
  1. Validate installation:
$thumb = (Get-PfxCertificate ".\ca.crt").Thumbprint
Get-ChildItem "Cert:\LocalMachine\Root" | Where-Object { $_.Thumbprint -eq $thumb }

Linux (manual commands)

Debian/Ubuntu/Alpine style:

cd ~/Downloads/certs
sudo cp ca.crt /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/ca.crt
sudo update-ca-certificates

RHEL/CentOS/Fedora style:

cd ~/Downloads/certs
sudo cp ca.crt /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/ca.crt
sudo update-ca-trust extract

Quick validation:

openssl x509 -in ca.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256

macOS (manual commands)

  1. Open Terminal.
  2. Go to the extracted cert folder:
cd ~/Downloads/certs
  1. Install certificate into System keychain:
sudo security add-trusted-cert -d -r trustRoot -k /Library/Keychains/System.keychain ./ca.crt
  1. Validate certificate is present:
sudo security find-certificate -a -Z /Library/Keychains/System.keychain | grep -i "$(openssl x509 -in ./ca.crt -noout -fingerprint -sha256 | awk -F= '{print $2}' | tr -d ':')"

Use helper script

This project includes install_ca_cert.sh with cross-platform logic.

cd ~/Downloads/certs
chmod +x ./install_ca_cert.sh
./install_ca_cert.sh
Download script
